title: Mirror Column Summary (Sum for Mirrored Number column)
excerpt: We use the mirrored columns on monday to compare predicted numbers to actuals figures. The most annoying thing is that it doesn’t show/bring through the column totals like on a normal number column. It would be really ha…

posts.author: dplcharlotte (Charlotte Turner)
posts.cookedHtml: We use the mirrored columns on monday to compare predicted numbers to actuals figures. The most annoying thing is that it doesn’t show/bring through the column totals like on a normal number column. It would be really handy if this worked so we could compare on one board rather than having to flick between boards.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Charlotte, welcome to the Monday community!
I do not know if I understand you correctly. If you mean that you would like to see the total of multiple items from one board (the total coming from a number column on multiple items) implemented via a link to item-, and a mirror column in a new board, then I am totally agreeing. This would be seriously handy to quickly compare the sum multiple items on a different (more high level) board.

posts.author: JustinJ (Justin Junioous)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @dplcharlotte and @Eltjo,

Thanks for sharing your feedback. I think this is a great idea.

Just to clarify and ensure I pass along the right feature request, you are looking to add a footer to mirror columns that have numbers to add up the total?

We are working on adding a lot more functionality to our link and mirror column options so stay tuned and keep crushing it on monday.

Justin

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi JustinJ, good to hear that you guys are working on new functionality for the mirror column. We are using those columns more and more. I am not sure about the request from dplcharlotte but I can explain what I meant.

**We work with three multidisciplinaire agile development teams. These teams work in sprints of 2 weeks (see screenshot of the High Level Sprint Board).

Screenshot 2019-08-30 at 20.02.35.png3086×736 514 KB

In this board I use the link to column to get the items that are scheduled for this sprint from a backlog board of a project.
For example, in the image you now see that 4 items are planned in ‘sprint 18’ for the NLdoet project and 5 items for the Espero project.

The ‘Total …’ column that belongs to NLdoet displays 1.5. This is the number that belongs to the first of the four items. My wish would be to show here the total of the four items (which is a total of 11).

This gives me the possibility to link all items from the backlog board to this board under ‘sprint 18’, so that I know the total of the estimate of a backlog of a project.

The first column of this high level sprint planning board then displays the total of the different backlog boards (a sum of the two ‘total …’ columns on the right).

In addition, it would be very, very cool to display the average of the status in the ‘Average status’ column in my image. Like the ‘footer’ of the status column, or the battery of the dashboard.
In this way, as a project manager, I can see the status of multiple projects in one go on my high level sprint board.

Thanks! I hope my explanation is clear like this

posts.author: dplcharlotte (Charlotte Turner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JustinJ

That’s exactly what I mean! I am sometimes comparing the total of number of days from a mirrored column and cant see the total which is the slightly frustrating bit.

posts.author: ppilalas (Philip)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m just trying to figure out how you made the “average status” column… Also, being able to use the “totals” from a column would be awesome.

posts.author: CValverde (Carlos Valverde Montero)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Eltjo, @dplcharlotte, having the same problem. It would be great if mirrored number columns showed column totals like a normal number column. Thanks!

posts.author: jiminy (Jim Hourican)
posts.cookedHtml: Workaround: I don’t know if this supports your scenario, but here’s what I do. After creating the mirrored column, simply create a formula column that just refers to that column. That column will now have the summary line to sum, count, etc. I then hide the mirrored column and move it to the right end to keep it out of the way.

posts.author: jaacquees (Jacques Barouhiel)
posts.cookedHtml: I had the same need and have been using the same workaround, but it is a shame really to have to create a separate column. I have some boards where almost all columns are mirror columns, it doubles up the number of columns. @devteam: would it be an easy change to make?

posts.author: MonDude (MonDude)
posts.cookedHtml: I encountered this same thing: if you have a column of numbers on a board, you have a footer at the bottom that will display the total. But if you mirror that same column to another board, you have no ability to see the sum in a footer on the new board. Seems simple!

posts.author: rmwarner (Randy Warner)
posts.cookedHtml: How do you avoid the formula column from rounding up though? For instance, my mirrored column properly displays 6.5 (hours) in total but the formula column displays 7.

posts.author: giulliasacco (Giullia Sacco)
posts.cookedHtml:
The way I solved this is by adding an extra formula column in which I use as formula just the other formula, as you can see in the picture below.

image960×252 22.9 KB

The “Amount” column is the mirrored column from the other board. To make it sum, I created a new formula column “Registered” which only holds the formula “{€ Amount}”. And, as you can see, then the sum is seen as normal at the bottom.

image828×321 24.8 KB

Let me know if my answer helped you somehow.

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.

posts.author: Uriel (Uriel Klein)
posts.cookedHtml: If the mirrored column includes numbers, we should be able to see a summary of those numbers at the bottom of the group (when the group is collapsed)

posts.author: hanniella (Hannah)
posts.cookedHtml: 4 years later - it doesn’t look as though Sums are appeared with Mirrored number columns, which is such a shame! I do hope this is introduced soon.

posts.author: MonicaMarrs (Monica Marrs)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree. This is a necessity at this point. Please add this functionality as soon as you can, Monday.com!

posts.author: RENEE.L (RENEE)
posts.cookedHtml:
I use the same method of adding extra formula column just to get the sum total of a mirrored number column, but this could be avoided if the mirrored column can sum up a total itself, without creating formula columns…
Should be an easy function to add on by the Monday team experts!! Please let us know when this could be introduced!! We would love to see that! @monday-team

posts.author: CatalinBaciu (Catalin Baciu)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team bump, any idea if this will be implemented and when?

posts.author: rodneyz (Rodney)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, there is a limitation on using mirror columns, this is why a lot of people have just had the same item live on multiple boards. You can enable this by first installing Same Item Multiple Boards by Formula Lookout, then select the item you want to sync, then enable the app in the item view.

@hanniella @Uriel @MonicaMarrs @RENEE.L @CatalinBaciu

posts.author: ToninSambeat (Tonin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
I have a different issue tho.

I have items for events, then in subitems different promotional actions that show a summary of total costs for that event. But I want to see the grant total of all the events. Is not showing because the individual costs are taken from the subitem mirrored column…

Screenshot 2024-10-03 at 14.30.46441×713 28 KB
